raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The section makes specific causal and definitional claims—DG is attributed exclusively to the planning-protocol change, R(τ) is asserted to retain a full threshold-dependent profile beyond CVaR, and the cliff threshold is characterized as a binary market-eligibility gate—each of which requires checking for internal logical coherence and mutual consistency.","R1.6":"A bare 'review' delegation standardly covers prose quality; the section contains heavily nominalized, multi-clause constructions (e.g., 'the value at the cliff threshold gives the fraction of deployments that actually earn revenue') and abrupt paragraph transitions that are natural candidates for sentence-level polish."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is the review primarily aimed at verifying the technical and logical correctness of the definitions and causal claims (making R1.2 load-bearing), at improving prose clarity and paragraph flow (making R1.6 load-bearing), or both with equal priority?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section makes specific causal and definitional claims—DG is attributed exclusively to the planning-protocol change, R(τ) is asserted to retain a full threshold-dependent profile beyond CVaR, and the cliff threshold is characterized as a binary market-eligibility gate—each of which requires checking for internal logical coherence and mutual consistency.
rationale.R1.6: A bare 'review' delegation standardly covers prose quality; the section contains heavily nominalized, multi-clause constructions (e.g., 'the value at the cliff threshold gives the fraction of deployments that actually earn revenue') and abrupt paragraph transitions that are natural candidates for sentence-level polish.

parsed.clarification_question: Is the review primarily aimed at verifying the technical and logical correctness of the definitions and causal claims (making R1.2 load-bearing), at improving prose clarity and paragraph flow (making R1.6 load-bearing), or both with equal priority?
